BASCHERINI vs. USF, ET AL. [SFSC #CGC-18-567239]- PETITION FOR ORDER TO ARBITRATE;
FOR DISMISSAL OF COMPLAINT OR, STAY OF ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION
SACliont\101 USFI095 Bascherini\Pleadings\Petition to Compel Arbitration\Patition.dac

 
1 PETITION

2 Defendants University of San Francisco (“the University”) and Charles White (“Mr.

3 || White”) (collectively, “Defendants”) petition the Court for an Order to Arbitrate and for

4 ||Dismissal of the Complaint or, in the alternative for stay of the action pending contractual

5 |larbitration. The Petition is based on this Petition; the Memorandum of Points and Authorities;

6 ||the two declaration attached thereto; the complete papers, records and files in this action; and

7 || upon such other oral and/or documentary evidence or argument as may be presented at or prior

8 || to the hearing on this Petition.

9 The Petition is made pursuant to Federal Arbitration Act, 9 U.S.C. § 1, ef seq., California
10 || Code of Civil Procedure §§ 1281 and 1290.2, and California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1330, and
11 || on the grounds that:

12 1, Plaintiff Robert Bascherini (‘Plaintiff’) is party to a written agreement for
13 || contractual arbitration executed by Plaintiff and his employer, University of San Francisco, a
14 || fully accredited institution of higher education located in San Francisco, California. The written
15 || agreement (“Agreement”) was executed by Plaintiff and the University in 2012 and contains the
16 || following provisions regarding arbitration:
17 “Any and all legal disputes initiated by either you or the University must be submitted
18 only to binding neutral arbitration, before a mutually chosen arbitrator, and not to a
court, judge or jury. Both parties have the right to be represented by counsel of their
19 choice; each is entitled to discovery consistent with law; and to obtain those remedies for
claims and defenses as would be ordered by a court. The University will pay all of the
20 professional fees of the arbitrator unless you object. The arbitrator will issue a written
1 award that must be accompanied by a written statement of decision. Legal disputes
include those arising under this contract or under federal, state and/or local statutes; the
2 common law of California; all contract and tort and other legal matters; but excluding
unemployment benefits, workers’ compensation benefits, the Labor Management
23 Relations Act of 1947, and EEOC, DFEH, and/or State Labor Commissioner wage
complaints, which complaints may be filed with the government agencies
24 notwithstanding this arbitration provision.”
25 2. The allegations of Plaintiff's Complaint for Damages filed in this Court on June
26 || 13, 2018 give rise to a controversy arising out of the Agreement, requiring its interpretation or
27 ||application and/or otherwise relating to the Agreement. A true and correct copy of the
28 || Agreement is attached to the declaration of Michael J. Vartain filed herewith.
BASCHERINI vs. USF, ET AL. [SFSC #CGC-18-567239|— PETITION FOR ORDER TO ARBITRATE;
FOR DISMISSAL OF COMPLAINT OR, STAY OF ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION
$:\Client\101 USF\095 Bascherini\Pleadings\Patition to Compe! Arbitration\Petition.doc -1-

 
1 3. Plaintiff's Complaint alleges five causes of action against Defendants the
2 || University and Mr. White. The causes of action include 1) wrongful termination in violation of
3 || public policy, 2) race discrimination and 3) retaliation in violation of Californian Government
4 || Code section 12940, 4) defamation, and 5) intentional infliction of emotional distress.
5 4. Plaintiff's Complaint alleges that Mr. White was “at all times relevant the Senior
6 ||Director of Recreational Sports at USF.” Plaintiff's Complaint alleges that
7 ||“each of the Defendants herein was the agent, servant, licensee, employee or alter ego, of each
8 || of the remaining Defendants, and was at all times herein mentioned acting within the course and
9 || scope of said relationship.”
10 5. The University’s operation as an institution of higher education affects
11 ||commerce or is in commerce within the meaning of the United States Federal Arbitration Act.
12 || Among its operations affecting or in commerce are: 1) Annual revenues and the interstate
13 || sources of those revenues (i.e., federal funding that derives from or is guaranteed by the United
14 ||States Treasury, and funding from tuition payments by out of state and foreign students and
15 || alumni); 2) interstate and foreign composition of the University’s student body; 3) interstate and
16 || foreign academia programs; and 4) interstate and foreign travel by the University’s faculty and
17 || staff employees.
18 6. Mr. White is an employee of the University who is entitled under law to the
19 || benefits of and enforcement of the arbitration provisions of the Agreement as to the Complaint
20 || in its entirety.
21 7. Plaintiff is equitably estopped from refusing to arbitrate this dispute with Mr.
22 || White as to the Complaint in its entirety.
23 8. On June 21, 2018, Defendants demanded of Plaintiff that he agree to arbitrate the
24 || controversy and dismiss the action.
25 9. Plaintiff refused the demand to arbitrate the controversy and to dismiss the action
26 || by the failure of his counsel to agree to the demand to arbitrate.
27 10. Defendants are ready and willing to submit the controversy alleged in the
28 || Complaint to arbitration.
BASCHERINI vs. USF, ET AL. [SFSC #CGC-18-567239]~ PETITION FOR ORDER TO ARBITRATE;
FOR DISMISSAL OF COMPLAINT OR, STAY OF ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION
§:\Client\101 USF\095 Bascherini\Pleadings\Patition to Compel Arbitration\Petition. doc -2-

 
1 WHEREFORE, Defendants pray for the following:
2 1. For an order compelling Plaintiff to arbitrate the controversy alleged in his
3 || Complaint; and
4 2. For an order dismissing the Complaint with prejudice or, in the alternative, for an
5 || order staying the action pending contractual arbitration.
6
